text: Cryptographic hash functions map arbitrary data to fixed-size digests (fingerprints). Key properties: preimage resistance (cannot reverse), second preimage resistance (cannot find different input with same hash), collision resistance (cannot find any two inputs with same hash). SHA-256 (32 bytes) is the current standard; SHA-3 (Keccak) is the next
SHA-2 family: SHA-224, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512 (all considered secure).
SHA-3 is not a replacement but an alternative with different internal structure (sponge construction).
Password hashing uses specialized functions: bcrypt (adaptive cost), scrypt (memory-hard), Argon2 (winner of PHC, 2015 — memory-hard + side-channel resistant).
